COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Jul-30-2025 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Damon Carter; Dept. 505
Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:30am breaking at 10:42am resuming at 11:07am and concluding at 11:31am.

Plaintiff John Brosnan appeared in court.
Defendant Sanford Troy appeared via zoom.

Mr. Troy did not respond to requests to turn on his audio. Mr. Troy did not contact the department via phone or zoom chat to state he was having technical difficulties.

The court proceeds with the default hearing

Counsel for Plaintiff presents summary of case and an offer of proof.

The matter is submitted.

Based on the documents filed, the statement made by Plaintiff and its Counsel, consideration and good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff John Brosnan, and against Defendant Sanford Troy, as follows:

Judgement from Serani Law Suit: $13,275.00
Prayer demand from Serani Law Suit: $250,000.00
Attorney Fees: $6,755.00
Failure to provide a retainer agreement: $1000.00

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $271,030.71

Mr. Brosnan is to prepare a judgment and submit it to this department for review and signature.

MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PROTECTIVE ORDER

No response to the court's order to show cause having been made, the court issues a protective order that requires plaintiff John Brosnan to obtain leave of the court prior to serving any subpoena on nonparty Bernie Schachter or any motion seeking any relief against Schachter.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT (CONTINUED)
Off Calendar Apr-08-2025 Continued To Apr-21-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-07-2025 Continued To Apr-08-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Require Bernie Schachter ("Bs") To Return Equipment Of Jb, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Mar-24-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Plaintiff JOHN BROSNAN, in pro per present.
Atty. STEPHEN ABRAHAM, Esq. of Law Offices of Stephen Abraham (tel: 949-878-8608) for Objector Bernie Schachter.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF JOHN BROSNAN's Motion To Require Bernie Schachter ("Bs") To Return Equipment Of Jb.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Order denying motion to compel:

Plaintiff John Brosnan's motion to compel Bernie Schachter to return certain equipment is denied. Brosnan cites no statutory basis for the motion. Schachter is not a party in this case and the only basis to compel him to produce things or otherwise be subject to the court's orders is through third-party discovery. Brosnan makes no showing that he has served such discovery. More importantly, he has not shown specific facts establishing good cause for his motion, and he has not shown that his requests to Schachter are reasonably calculcated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence regarding his claims against the defendant in the present case.

Brosnan has previously been declared a vexatious litigant and was granted leave to file this negligence action against his former counsel, Sanford Troy. He is subject to a prefiling order and the court granted leave to him to file an action against Troy, not against Schachter. (Order, Mar. 21, 2024.) Schachter asks the court to revoke Brosnan's prefiling leave. But Brosnan's unsuccessful motions against Schachter do not go to merits of the case Brosnan filed against Troy. The court denies this request for relief.

Notice of order to show cause:

Sua sponte, the court issues an order to show cause to Brosnan. Brosnan must show cause why a protective order should not be entered that would require him to obtain leave of the court prior to serving any subpoena on Schachter and prior to filing a new motion seeking any relief against Schachter. Brosnan shall show cause why such an order should not be entered at hearing on April 21, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 301. He may file a response to the OSC by five court days before the hearing or may show cause at the hearing.

The basis for this order to show cause is as follows. As a vexatious litigant, Brosnan is subject to a prefiling order and his leave to proceed with the complaint in this case does not give him leave to seek relief from non-parties whom he has not obtained permission to proceed against. The items he seeks from Schachter through this motion appear related to the previous litigation he pursued against Schachter unsuccessfully. (Abraham Dec. paras. 4-25; Order, Feb. 21, 2025.) Moreover, any motions he files against a nonparty could only properly relate to discovery. The court has previously denied his motion to compel compliance with a subpoena against Schachter. (See Order, Feb. 21, 2025.) Schachter did not receive Brosnan's moving papers by the means Brosnan's proof of service attests. (Abraham Dec. paras. 1-3.) The court is concerned that Brosnan is using the present litigation to harass or burden Schachter. He does not have prefiling leave to pursue claims against Schachter. The court has discretion to supervise discovery, enter protective orders, and mitigate litigation burdens on third parties. (See Greyhound Corp. v. Superior Court (1961) 56 Cal.2d 355 ["the discovery statutes vest a wide discretion on the trial court in granting or denying discovery"]; Calcor Space Facility, Inc. v. Superior Court (1997) 53 Cal.App.4th 216, 225 ["As between parties to litigation and nonparties, the burden of discovery should be placed on the latter only if the former do not possess the material sought to be discovered."].)

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Bernie Schachter, And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Feb-21-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF JOHN BROSNAN's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Bernie Schachter.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion to compel Bernie Schachter's compliance with Plaintiff's deposition subpoena is denied.

In Plaintiff's moving papers, filed on January 23, 2025, he provides a proof of service of deposition subpoena on Schachter that is dated August 21, 2024. (Opening Br. at 5.) The date specified for deposition was September 20, 2024. (Abraham Dec. para 33.) Schachter objected on August 22 and did not appear. The present motion was filed more than 60 days after the date of objections and the date of the deposition and is therefore untimely. (CCP 2025.480(b).)

Plaintiff's reply brief states that he has now served a new deposition subpoena on Schachter, apparently seeking the same items. That subpoena is not properly before the court, but even assuming Plaintiff could cure his delay in moving to compel, the court finds that Schachter's objections have merit. Plaintiff has not filed a separate statement in support of his motion. (See Rule of Court 3.1345.) He has not stated any good cause why the court should compel production; his reply brief states why he should have prevailed in the already-litigated action Brosnan v. JSB Equities LP et al., but does not discuss the relevance of Schachter's testimony or the items he has sought through subpoena to his new action against his former attorney.

CCP 2025.480(j) provides that the court "shall" impose a monetary sanction on any party who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a discovery motion like the one at issue unless it finds that the party acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make imposition of sanction unjust. The court finds that Brosnan acted without substantial justification here but reduces the amount awarded in light of all the circumstances here. The court awards $3,000 in sanctions to Schachter, payable by Plaintiff via Schachter's attorney, within 30 days of notice of entry of this order.

Schachter also seeks a stay of discovery against him. The court denies this request without prejudice to any properly noticed motion for protective order.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Off Calendar Oct-02-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOHN BROSNAN MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY/RESPONSE TO DISCOVERY

The unopposed motion is denied without prejudice as it did not provide sufficient notice (16 court days counting back from date of hearing plus 5 calendar days for service by mail) pursuant to C.C.P. 1005. It is also denied for lack of showing that when Plaintiff's First Set of Special Interrogatories was served upon Defendant which renders judicial review impossible.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem williamclynn@gmail.com.
